finalized_plan.description: Use a GitHub search query to filter closed issues in the numpy/numpy repository for those related to the numpy.polynomial component and carrying the “Regression” label
Review the search results to identify candidate issues and sort them by creation or closing date to find the oldest candidate meeting the criteria

For the leading candidate (e.g., issue #26843 if confirmed to be related to numpy.polynomial and have the Regression label), verify that the issue is indeed closed and associated with the numpy.polynomial component
Access the candidate issue’s timeline or events (via GitHub’s API endpoint or an alternative timeline URL) to locate the event log where the “Regression” label was added
Locate the specific timeline event indicating the addition of the “Regression” label and extract its timestamp
Convert the extracted timestamp into MM/DD/YY format
Verify that no earlier qualifying issue exists and that the candidate selected is indeed the oldest meeting all required criteria
Compile the findings and prepare a final answer with: (a) a short outcome summary, (b) an extremely detailed explanation of the steps and reasoning, and (c) additional context if relevant

root_cause_reasoning: The target webpage was not found despite multiple search attempts.
notes: Controversial! Whether to perform search directly or to call the ToolCallingAgent
